Control Description: The objective specified in ISO/IEC 27002:2013, 5.1 applies.
Define, approve, and annually review an enterprise cybersecurity policy covering scope, roles, risk appetite, and control baselines.
Assign accountable owners for cybersecurity, define RACI, and ensure separation of duties for administration and audit.
Adopt recognized security controls framework and tailor to organizational risk (e.g., benchmark or regulatory mapping).
Perform periodic risk assessments, document risks, treatment plans, and track residual risk.
Identify applicable laws/regulations and monitor compliance posture and remediation.
Formal process to record, approve, time-bound, and monitor security exceptions.
Assess and monitor third-party cybersecurity, include security clauses and right-to-audit.
Define BIA, RTO/RPO targets, recovery strategies, and test plans annually.
Implement DR for critical systems, perform failover tests, and validate data integrity.
Define incident classifications, runbooks, communication, and escalation procedures; test semi-annually.
Preserve logs and chain-of-custody for investigations; ensure legal admissibility.
Enforce MFA for privileged and user access; disable legacy protocols where possible.
Use role-based access, just-in-time elevation, and periodic access reviews.
Manage keys/secrets in hardened vaults, rotate, and enforce CMK for sensitive workloads.
Eliminate local/shared keys and legacy admin accounts; require identity-based auth.
Segment networks, isolate sensitive workloads, enforce east-west controls.
Disable public endpoints and require Private Link/private endpoints for data services.
Use Azure Firewall/WAF, DDoS Standard, and secure ingress/egress.
Require TLS 1.2+, enforce HTTPS-only, and disable insecure ciphers.
Classify data, apply labels, and enforce protection policies by sensitivity.
Encrypt data at rest with CMK where required; enforce TLS in transit.
Apply DLP across channels; monitor and block sensitive data exfiltration.
Back up critical data, validate restorability, and protect backup immutability.
Enable diagnostic logs and forward to a central SIEM/Log Analytics.
Enable cloud workload posture monitoring and real-time alerts.
Define log retention, audit coverage, and tamper-evident storage.
Continuously assess and patch VMs, containers, databases, and apps.
Threat modeling, code scanning, dependency management (SBOM), and secure builds.
Formal change approval, peer review, and rollback strategies.
Assess model risks, document intended use, and test for harmful behavior.
Control prompts/responses, apply DLP, and safeguard training/grounding data.
Place AI services in private networks, restrict outbound, and protect secrets.
Require Customer Lockbox and no data persistence for sensitive AI workloads.
Ensure DLP and sensitivity labels protect data used by Copilot; restrict summarization of highly confidential items.
Enable auditing and retention for Copilot interactions and outputs as applicable.
Include AI-generated content and interactions in eDiscovery and legal hold.
Monitor Copilot usage for inappropriate or noncompliant content.
Require multi-factor authentication for all accounts

Use managed identities for Azure resources
Storage accounts should prevent Shared Key authorization
Key Vault should use RBAC authorization
Key Vault should restrict network access and use Private Endpoints
Services should use customer-managed keys
Public network access should be disabled
Subnets should be protected with NSGs; use Azure Firewall for egress control
Enable DDoS Protection Plan for internet-facing workloads
Resources should enforce HTTPS and minimum TLS version
Encrypt managed disks and storage accounts; CMK where required
Enable Azure Backup for VMs and critical workloads
Enable Microsoft Purview for data classification and labels
Implement DLP for data channels (M365, endpoints, Azure AI egress)
Deploy diagnostic settings to send logs to Log Analytics
Enable Defender for Cloud enhanced security plans
Set retention for activity logs and workspace data
Enable vulnerability assessment on VMs, containers, and SQL
Apply Guest Configuration baselines (Windows/Linux)
Require tags and approved resource types
Cognitive Services/Azure ML should disable public network access; restrict outbound to approved FQDNs
Enable Purview policy for AI apps; govern prompts/responses
Enable content safety and perform red-teaming for LLM apps
Require Customer Lockbox for support access to data
Create DLP policy scoped to Microsoft 365 Copilot to block summarization of highly confidential items
Enable auditing of Copilot interactions and retention policies
Include Copilot outputs and interactions in eDiscovery cases and legal holds
Enable Communication Compliance for Copilot-generated content
Cognitive Services accounts should use customer-managed keys
Public network access should be disabled (Cognitive Services)
Enable Customer Lockbox for support access
Restrict outbound network access; allowlist required FQDNs
Restrict models and connections via Azure Policy/custom guardrails
Public network access should be disabled; workspace private endpoints
Use CMK for AML; Key Vault with firewall + RBAC
Restrict outbound connections for compute clusters
Public network access should be disabled; use Private Link
Web apps should only be accessible over HTTPS; use MI for secrets
Create policy scoped to Microsoft 365 Copilot to block summarization of Highly Confidential
Ensure Copilot honors EXTRACT/VIEW rights; configure label inheritance for generated content (where supported)
Enable auditing for Copilot interactions; configure retention for AI outputs
Include Copilot-generated content in holds and cases
Monitor Copilot content for policy violations
